But I need to make sure that the command is correctly formatted. The -exec option in find takes a command, which in this case is a shell command. The "$1" is the directory being processed. The command inside the -exec is 'find "$1" -type d | wc -l', which finds all subdirectories under the current directory and counts them.

However, the -exec option in find requires that the command is properly formatted. So, the correct command would be:

arguments.command: find /testdata/system -type d -exec sh -c 'find "$1" -type d | wc -l' \; | sort -nr | head -n 10
